us-politics.headline: US-Iran War Escalates as Trump Threatens Deeper Strikes
us-politics.body:
Iran struck Gulf oil refineries this week, and Trump responded by warning the US military would hit Iranian bridges and power plants. He told reporters the military "hasn't even started" destroying what remains in Iran. Investors appear unconvinced the conflict will resolve quickly, with markets showing little movement after Trump's prime-time address.

The US has deployed B-52 bombers to the region, and troops are being evacuated from a key Mideast base in Bahrain, sending military families and community groups scrambling. Iran's grip on the Strait of Hormuz gives it real leverage over global oil flows, complicating Trump's position. Families of slain US service members, Trump says, urged him to finish the fight.

sources.title: Trump: US military ‘hasn’t even started destroying what’s left in Iran’
Iran hits Gulf refineries as Trump warns U.S. will attack Iranian bridges, power plants
Iran ‘toll booth’ on Strait of Hormuz underscores its leverage on Trump, US
What B-52 bombers bring to Iran fight — and what it means for the war now
WATCH: President Trump reveals families of slain US service members urged him ‘finish the job’
Evacuation of U.S. troops from Mideast base sends community groups scrambling to help

us-politics.headline: Bondi Fired: DOJ Shake-Up Raises Epstein and Voter Data Questions
us-politics.body:
Trump fired Attorney General Pam Bondi, with analysts pointing to her failure to deliver high-profile prosecutions Trump wanted. Trump's lawyer Todd Blanche says he never heard Trump link her exit to the Epstein files, but the timing has drawn scrutiny. Democrats, including Rep. Ro Khanna, are pressing for answers on whether the Epstein inquiry will continue under new leadership.

Separately, the DOJ is preparing to share state voter data with the Department of Homeland Security. A key DOJ privacy officer resigned ahead of that transfer, raising alarms among civil liberties advocates. Five major questions now hang over the department: who leads it next, what happens to ongoing investigations, and whether political priorities will reshape federal law enforcement.

sources.title: Blanche: I never heard Trump say Bondi exit ‘had anything to do with the Epstein files’
Whole Hog Politics: Bondi beached
Rep. Ro Khanna, D-Calif., on the future of the Epstein inquiry with Bondi's ousting
Five questions hanging over the DOJ shake-up
The Memo: Bondi’s failure to get Trump his ‘scalps’ sealed her fate
After the release of the Epstein files, why have there been so few arrests?
As DOJ prepares to share state voter data with DHS, a key privacy officer resigns

us-politics.headline: Jobs Beat Forecasts, but Political Headwinds Mount for Trump
us-politics.body:
The US added 178,000 jobs in March, beating expectations and giving the White House a rare positive headline in a turbulent week. Rising gas prices tied to the Iran conflict and broader economic uncertainty have otherwise dented Trump's standing, with analysts noting the war's costs are starting to register with voters.

Elsewhere, VP Vance is set to meet Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán days before Hungary's parliamentary elections, a visit that will draw fresh scrutiny over the administration's ties to nationalist European leaders. In Maine, Democratic political outsider Graham Platner is gaining ground in a Senate race, reflecting broader Democratic energy heading into the midterms.

sources.title: US adds 178K jobs in March, far more than expected
Trump pushed the Iran war in prime time. Investors weren’t swayed.
VP Vance to meet with Viktor Orbán in Hungary days ahead of foreign nation's elections
Graham Platner’s political outsider vibe gains steam in Maine among Democrats
Takeaways from Trump's tough week, as war and gas prices take a toll

us-politics.headline: Civil Rights, Campus Probes, and Union Laws Stir Domestic Fights
us-politics.body:
A lawsuit alleges the Congressional Black Caucus Foundation's scholarship program discriminates by race, a case that could test the boundaries of race-conscious giving after recent Supreme Court rulings. Separately, the Trump administration demanded a list of Jewish faculty at the University of Pennsylvania as part of an antisemitism probe, drawing sharp criticism from advocacy groups who called it "incredibly chilling."

A watchdog group is calling for a federal investigation into a military Apache helicopter flyby over Kid Rock's home, which critics say was an improper use of military assets. On labor policy, a Hill opinion piece contrasts Florida's tightened public-sector union rules with Oregon's move to expand them, illustrating the widening gap between red and blue state governance.

sources.title: Black Caucus scholarships discriminate based on race, lawsuit alleges
‘Incredibly chilling’: Advocacy groups raise alarm over Trump’s demand for list of Jewish faculty at Penn
Watchdog group calls for federal investigation into Apache flyby at Kid Rock’s house
Florida made public-sector unions more accountable — Oregon did the opposite

au-politics.headline: Australia's Fuel Crisis Deepens as Industry Sounds Alarm
au-politics.body:
Industry groups warn Australia's fuel reserves would last less than a month if supply chains were disrupted, putting pressure on the Albanese government to act. Energy Minister Chris Bowen has faced criticism that proposed solutions - including domestic refining incentives and strategic reserve expansion - fall short of what is needed.

The warning lands against a broader backdrop: the Iran conflict has tightened global oil markets, with workers across Asia already feeling the squeeze at the pump. Critics from the right argue net-zero policy commitments have left Western nations dangerously exposed to fossil fuel volatility, while others say the crisis underscores the urgency of transitioning away from oil dependency altogether.

sources.title: Fuel heading to Australia won't last a month, industry says
A day in the life of Asia’s fuel crisis
‘Not a fix’: the three questionable ideas politicians are pitching to solve Australia’s fuel crisis
Net Zero nations trapped in mad race for oil

au-politics.headline: Trump Fires Senior Officials in Sweeping Administration Purge
au-politics.body:
Donald Trump has dismissed a string of senior officials, including Attorney-General Pam Bondi, in what analysts describe as a broad consolidation of loyalty-based governance. The removals have drawn sharp criticism from former US officials, with one ex-envoy calling Trump's recent Iran speech "delusional" and warning it undermines diplomatic credibility.

Australia is watching closely. The government confirmed it will not raise domestic drug prices in response to Trump's 100% tariff on pharmaceuticals imported into the US, a move that could strain trade relations. The tariff announcement adds to existing uncertainty around the bilateral relationship.

sources.title: Donald Trump is going on a firing spree
Trump's fiercest female defenders are now on the outer
'Every word is delusional': Trump's speech lashed by former US envoy to Iran
Australia says it won’t raise drug prices after Trump’s 100% tariff on pharmaceuticals imported into US

au-politics.headline: NSW Teens Charged Over Terror Material in Easter Weekend Arrests
au-politics.body:
Two NSW teenagers have been charged over violent extremist material allegedly linked to terrorism, in arrests that come as Australian security agencies maintain heightened vigilance heading into the Easter long weekend. Details of the alleged material and any specific targets have not been publicly released.

Separately, a Queensland man was sentenced to 15 years in prison for live-streaming child sexual abuse, a case prosecutors described as among the most serious of its kind. Both cases highlight ongoing pressure on law enforcement to respond to crimes facilitated or amplified by online platforms.

sources.title: Two NSW teenagers charged over violent extremist material allegedly linked to terror
Queensland man jailed for 15 years after live-streaming child abuse

au-politics.headline: Easter Road Toll Mounts as Crashes Kill Multiple Australians
au-politics.body:
At least four people have died in separate crashes across Australia over the Easter long weekend. A 14-year-old dirt bike rider was among three killed in Western Australia, while motorcyclists died in crashes on the Monaro Highway in NSW and at a traffic light intersection in Adelaide.

Authorities have renewed calls for caution on roads during the holiday period. The deaths come as Australians otherwise marked Good Friday with church services, seafood and the opening of the new Sydney Fish Market, which drew large crowds on its first major holiday since opening.

sources.title: Dirt bike rider, 14, among three dead in separate WA Easter long weekend crashes
Highway closed after motorcyclist dies in crash in southern NSW
Motorcyclist killed in traffic light crash in Adelaide, another rushed to hospital
Australians mark Good Friday with seafood, church services and a boat race
Packed to the gills: shoppers embrace new Sydney Fish Market during Good Friday rush

world.headline: US-Israeli War on Iran Enters Day 35 Amid War Crimes Allegations
world.body:
US and Israeli forces struck civilian infrastructure in Iran on day 35 of the conflict, hitting a bridge in Karaj and drawing condemnation from Tehran over what it called a 'moral collapse.' Over 100 American legal experts have formally alleged the strikes constitute war crimes under international law, adding pressure on the Trump administration to define its endgame.

Iranians on the ground express deepening fear about escalation, while analysts question whether Washington has a coherent timeline for ending the war. Kuwait also reported missile and drone strikes on a desalination plant and oil refinery, signalling the conflict is spreading beyond Iran's borders and destabilising the wider region.

sources.title: War crimes are no longer shameful. That should terrify you
International law experts allege violations in Iran war
When will the Iran war end? Tracing the Trump administration's timelines
Over 100 US legal experts condemn strikes on Iran as possible ‘war crimes’
'This has got me worried': Iranians fear what comes next after US strike on Karaj bridge
Kuwait desalination plant, oil refinery hit by missile and drone strikes
Iran condemns US-Israeli ‘moral collapse’ after attacks on civilian sites
Iran war: What is happening on day 35 of US-Israeli attacks?

world.headline: Trump Fires Bondi, Hegseth Purges Army Leadership
world.body:
President Trump removed Attorney General Pam Bondi and replaced her with Todd Blanche, his former personal defence lawyer. The dismissal follows a pattern of loyalty-driven reshuffles across the administration.

Separately, Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth fired the US Army Chief of Staff as part of a reported string of military dismissals. General Christopher LaNeve is set to take command of the Army. The back-to-back purges of both civilian and military leadership in a single news cycle raise fresh questions about institutional stability at the top of the US government.

sources.title: Who is Christopher LaNeve, set to lead the US army?
Trump removes US Attorney General Pam Bondi
Why did Trump fire Pam Bondi from Justice Department, who is Todd Blanche?
Hegseth fires US Army chief of staff in reported string of dismissals

world.headline: Burkina Faso Rejects Democracy; Myanmar Coup Leader Becomes President
world.body:
Burkina Faso's military ruler Ibrahim Traore told citizens to 'forget democracy,' cementing the junta's grip after seizing power in 2022. The statement marks one of the bluntest rejections of democratic governance by any sitting leader in recent memory.

In Myanmar, coup leader Min Aung Hlaing formally assumed the presidency, consolidating power more than four years after the military overthrew the elected government. Both moves reflect a broader authoritarian consolidation across two continents, as international pressure on each regime has largely failed to produce political change.

sources.title: Burkina Faso military leader Traore says ‘forget democracy’
Burkina Faso must 'forget' about democracy, military leader says
Myanmar's coup leader who set off a brutal civil war becomes president

world.headline: Artemis II Breaks Earth Orbit, Heads for Far Side of Moon
world.body:
NASA's Artemis II crew fired the spacecraft's engines and successfully left Earth's orbit, putting four astronauts on course for the far side of the Moon. It is the first crewed lunar mission since Apollo 17 in 1972.

The astronauts spoke from space as the mission hit its early milestones on schedule. A successful Artemis II would set the stage for a crewed lunar landing under Artemis III, marking a generational leap in human spaceflight.

sources.title: Artemis II leaves Earth's orbit on track for far side of the Moon
Artemis II mission fires up engines in next step of moon mission
Watch: Artemis II astronauts speak from space

ai.headline: OpenAI Buys Media Outlet, Cuts ChatGPT Business Price
ai.body:
OpenAI acquired TBPN, a founder-focused tech talk show with a loyal startup audience, in a move that effectively purchases a sympathetic media platform. Wired called it buying positive news coverage. The deal raises obvious questions about editorial independence that OpenAI has not answered.

Separately, OpenAI quietly lowered the price of ChatGPT Business, signaling pressure in the enterprise market as competitors crowd in. Together, the two moves suggest a company working hard on both perception and pricing to hold its dominant position.

sources.title: Lower Price for ChatGPT Business
OpenAI Acquires Tech Talk Show ‘TBPN’—and Buys Itself Some Positive News
OpenAI acquires TBPN, the buzzy founder-led business talk show

ai.headline: AI Chatbots Now Prescribing Psychiatric Drugs
ai.body:
Chatbots are being used to prescribe and refill psychiatric medications, The Verge reports, marking a sharp escalation in AI's role in clinical care. These are not simple triage tools - they are making consequential decisions about drugs that carry serious risks and require careful monitoring.

Meanwhile, Granola's AI note-taking app was found to share notes publicly by default with anyone holding a link, and Perplexity faces a lawsuit alleging its incognito mode collects data it promised not to. The pattern is consistent: AI products moving fast, with privacy and safety guardrails trailing behind.

sources.title: Chatbots are now prescribing psychiatric drugs
PSA: Anyone with a link can view your Granola notes by default
Perplexity's "Incognito Mode" is a "sham," lawsuit says

ai.headline: EU Folds on Digital Rules Under US Pressure
ai.body:
The European Union is drawing fierce criticism after backing down on digital regulations following pressure from the United States. Critics are calling it a fatal decision that undermines years of work to rein in dominant US tech platforms. The retreat hands American companies significant leverage they had been lobbying hard to secure.

The backlash is fueling demand for European tech alternatives. A new directory listing EU-based replacements for Google, Apple, Dropbox and over 120 other American apps has surfaced on Hacker News, reflecting a growing appetite for digital sovereignty that the EU's own institutions appear unwilling to protect.

sources.title: 'Fatal decision': EU slammed for caving to US pressure on digital rules
Show HN: European alternatives to Google, Apple, Dropbox and 120 US apps

ai.headline: Rowhammer Attack Hands Attackers Full GPU Machine Control
ai.body:
Researchers have demonstrated new Rowhammer attacks that exploit Nvidia GPUs to gain complete control of a target machine. The technique manipulates memory at the hardware level, bypassing software defenses. Nvidia GPUs are now ubiquitous in AI infrastructure, which makes the attack surface unusually broad and the stakes unusually high.

Google is compounding infrastructure concerns from a different angle, funding a new data center that will draw power from a large gas plant. As AI compute demand surges, the energy trade-offs are becoming harder to ignore.

sources.title: New Rowhammer attacks give complete control of machines running Nvidia GPUs
A New Google-Funded Data Center Will Be Powered by a Massive Gas Plant

btc.headline: Bitcoin Drifts Into Holiday Weekend With Bears in Control
btc.body:
Bitcoin enters the Good Friday long weekend in a fragile position. ETF inflows and CME futures trading go dark for the holiday, stripping away two of the market's key demand signals at a moment when on-chain data shows the share of supply held in profit has fallen to levels analysts associate with true bear markets. Futures markets are already tilting bearish, and volatility has cooled rather than resolved.

Cathie Wood is pushing back on the gloom, arguing Bitcoin is done with the 85% drawdowns that defined earlier cycles and setting a $34,000 floor target. But with a strong March jobs report adding macro uncertainty and institutional flow channels offline until Tuesday, the path of least resistance looks downward through the weekend.

sources.title: U.S. March jobs smash expectations, with 178,000 added
Crypto snoozes into Good Friday as oil and macro stir: Crypto Daybook Americas
Crypto consolidates as volatility cools and futures markets tilt bearish
Bitcoin supply in profit heads to ‘true bear market’ levels
Bitcoin heads into holiday weekend exposed as ETF and CME flows go offline
Bitcoin 'done' with 85% crashes, says Cathie Wood amid new $34K target

btc.headline: Ethereum Foundation Hits Staking Target; Stablecoins Flip ACH Volume
btc.body:
The Ethereum Foundation completed its staking push, deploying a final tranche to reach its 70,000 ETH target worth roughly $93 million. The move signals a more active treasury posture from an organization historically cautious about staking its own holdings.

Separately, stablecoins surpassed ACH payment network volume in February, a milestone that puts crypto rails ahead of a system that processes trillions in U.S. payroll and bill payments annually. Circle is pressing further into the space, announcing cirBTC, a wrapped Bitcoin token aimed at institutional markets and positioned to compete directly with BitGo's WBTC and Coinbase's cbBTC. The IMF, meanwhile, acknowledged tokenization improves financial efficiency but warned it introduces new systemic risks.

sources.title: Ethereum Foundation stakes another $93 million ether, reaching its 70,000 ETH target
Stablecoins flip automated clearing house volume in February
Circle to launch cirBTC wrapped Bitcoin, challenging BitGo and Coinbase
Tokenization makes finance more efficient but introduces risks: IMF

btc.headline: DeFi Lost $169M to Hacks in Q1; Drift Contacts Its Exploiter On-Chain
btc.body:
DeFi protocols shed $169 million across 34 exploits in the first quarter of 2026, according to DefiLlama data, though the figure marks a decline from prior periods. Drift Protocol took an unusual step after its $280 million exploit, sending a public on-chain message directly to wallets linked to the attacker in an attempt to open negotiations.

A Bank of Canada study on Aave added nuance to how DeFi handles stress, finding that Aave V3 avoided bad debt during volatile periods by effectively pushing losses onto borrowers through aggressive liquidation mechanics rather than absorbing them at the protocol level. The findings raise questions about who actually bears risk in decentralized lending systems marketed as transparent and neutral.

sources.title: Crypto hackers steal $169M from 34 DeFi protocols in Q1: DefiLlama
Drift sends onchain message to wallets tied to $280M exploit
Aave avoided bad debt by shifting risk to borrowers: Bank of Canada study

btc.headline: Quantum-Resistant Chain Goes Live as Q-Day Threat Grows
btc.body:
Naoris Protocol launched its quantum-resistant blockchain on Thursday, timing the release to growing concern that advances in quantum computing could eventually break the cryptographic foundations of Bitcoin and Ethereum. The threat, sometimes called Q-Day, remains years away by most estimates, but the race to build resistant infrastructure is accelerating.

Elsewhere in infrastructure, South Korean brokerage Korea Investment and Securities is reportedly eyeing a stake in crypto exchange Coinone, a sign that traditional financial firms in Asia continue to push into regulated crypto venues. Big Tech firms also backed the new x402 Foundation, a protocol designed to advance payment rails for autonomous AI agents operating on-chain.

sources.title: Naoris Protocol's quantum-resistant blockchain goes live as Bitcoin and Ethereum face 'Q-Day' threats
South Korean brokerage Korea Investment & Securities eyes Coinone stake: Report
Big Tech firms back new x402 Foundation to advance agentic AI adoption

food.name: Miso-Glazed Black Cod
food.description: Silky, caramelised fish fillets marinated in white miso, mirin, and sake then broiled until lacquered and golden
food.tip: Marinate for at least 24 hours — the longer the miso sits on the fish, the deeper the umami crust when it hits the heat

exercise.name: Deck of Cards Conditioning
exercise.equipment: One standard deck of playing cards, a mat
exercise.description: Assign a bodyweight move to each suit — push-ups, squats, lunges, hollow-body holds — then flip cards one at a time and perform the reps shown, turning a random deck into an unpredictable full-body circuit

learn.topic: The Ship of Theseus and Personal Identity
learn.description: The ancient paradox asks whether an object that has had all its components replaced remains fundamentally the same object — and when applied to human cells, memories, and beliefs, it becomes a sharp question about whether 'you' today are the same person you were a decade ago.
learn.why: Philosophers from Heraclitus to Derek Parfit have used this puzzle to challenge intuitions about continuity and selfhood, and spending an hour with Parfit's 'Reasons and Persons' argument will permanently change how you think about identity, responsibility, and even death
